name: yarg

metadata.name: yarg
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: A semi hard Cornish cheese, also queries PyPI (PyPI client)

documentation:
# yarg(1) -- A semi hard Cornish cheese, also queries PyPI

```{image} https://img.shields.io/travis/kura/yarg.svg?style=flat
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/coveralls/kura/yarg.svg?style=flat
```

Yarg is a PyPI client.

```python
>>> import yarg
>>> package = yarg.get("yarg")
>>> package.name
u'yarg'
>>> package.author
Author(name=u'Kura', email=u'kura@kura.io')
```

Full documentation is at \<<https://yarg.readthedocs.org>>.

Yarg is released under the [MIT license](https://github.com/kura/yarg/blob/master/LICENSE). The [source code is on
GitHub](https://github.com/kura/yarg) and [issues are also tracked on
GitHub](https://github.com/kura/yarg/issues).

# Release History

0.1.10 (2024-08-09)
\- Python 3.12 support

## 0.1.9 (2014-08-11)

### Splatting bugs

- Added `decode` call on the response object for Python 3 in
  `yarg.newest_packages` and `yarg.latest_updated_packages`.

## 0.1.8 (2014-08-10)

### Splatting bugs

- Integration issue with Python 3, requests, yarg and JSON. Attempt to decode
  requests response if decode attribute exists.

## 0.1.6 & 0.1.7 (2014-08-10)

### Splatting bugs

- Bug in setup.py causing installs to fail for sdist (source) releases.

## 0.1.5 (2014-08-10)

### API changes

- Changed sort order of `yarg.package.Package.release_ids` to sort
  based on the upload time of the release ID.

### Splatting bugs

- `yarg.package.Package.latest_release_id` will now return the latest
  release ID from the PyPI info source, rather than the final list item in
  `yarg.package.Package.release_ids`.

  Addtionally `yarg.package.Package.latest_release` will do the same as
  it gets the latest release information from
  `yarg.package.Package.latest_release_id`.

## 0.1.4 (2014-08-09)

### API changes

- New method `yarg.newest_packages` for querying new packages
  from the PyPI RSS feed.
- New method `yarg.latest_updated_packages` for querying
  the latest updated packages from the PyPI RSS feed.

### Other

- Additional test coverage
- Additional documentation coverage

## 0.1.2 (2014-08-08)

### Bug fixes

- `yarg.get` will now raise an Exception for errors **including**
  300 and above. Previously only raised for above 300.
- Fix an issue on Python 3.X and PyPy3 where
  `yarg.exceptions.HTTPError` was using a method that was
  removed in Python 3.
- Added dictionary key lookups for `home_page`, `bugtrack_url`
  and `docs_url`. Caused `KeyError` exceptions if they were not
  returned by PyPI.

### Other

- More test coverage.

## 0.1.1 (2014-08-08)

### API changes

- New `yarg.package.Package` property `has_wheel`.
- New `yarg.package.Package` property `has_egg`.
- New `yarg.package.Package` property `has_source`.
- New `yarg.package.Package` property `python_versions`.
- New `yarg.package.Package` property `python_implementations`.
- Added `yarg.exceptions.HTTPError` to `yarg.__init__`
  for easier access.
- Added `yarg.json2package` to `yarg.__init__` to expose it for
  use.

## 0.1.0 (2014-08-08)

- Initial release

functions.name: yarg.client.get
documentation:
Construct a request to the PyPI server and returns an instance of
{class}`yarg.package.Package`.

```
param package_name

case sensitive name of the package on the PyPI server.

param pypi_server

(option) URL to the PyPI server.

>>> import yarg
>>> package = yarg.get('yarg')
<Package yarg>
```

modules.name: yarg
documentation:
yarg(1) -- A semi hard Cornish cheese, also queries PyPI
========================================================

Yarg is a PyPI client.

    >>> import yarg
    >>>
    >>> package = yarg.get("yarg")
    >>> package.name
    'yarg'
    >>> package.author
    Author(name='Kura', email='kura@kura.io')
    >>>
    >>> yarg.newest_packages()
    [<Package yarg>, <Package gray>, <Package ragy>]
    >>>
    >>> yarg.latest_updated_packages()
    [<Package yarg>, <Package gray>, <Package ragy>]

Full documentation is at <https://yarg.readthedocs.org>.

classes.name: yarg.exceptions.HTTPError
documentation:
A catchall HTTPError exception to handle HTTP errors
when using {meth}`yarg.get`.

This exception is also loaded at {class}`yarg.HTTPError`
for ease of access.

```
member

status_code
```

functions.name: yarg.parse._get
documentation:
Query the PyPI RSS feed and return a list
of XML items.

functions.name: yarg.parse.newest_packages
documentation:
Constructs a request to the PyPI server and returns a list of
{class}`yarg.parse.Package`.

```
param pypi_server

(option) URL to the PyPI server.

>>> import yarg
>>> yarg.newest_packages()
[<Package yarg>, <Package gray>, <Package ragy>]
```

functions.name: yarg.parse.latest_updated_packages
documentation:
Constructs a request to the PyPI server and returns a list of
{class}`yarg.parse.Package`.

```
param pypi_server

(option) URL to the PyPI server.

>>> import yarg
>>> yarg.latest_updated_packages()
[<Package yarg>, <Package gray>, <Package ragy>]
```

classes.name: yarg.parse.Package

methods.name: yarg.parse.Package.name
documentation:
```pycon
>>> package = yarg.newest_packages()[0]
>>> package.name
'yarg'
>>> package = yarg.latest_updated_packages()[0]
>>> package.name
'yarg'
```

methods.name: yarg.parse.Package.version
documentation:
```pycon
>>> package = yarg.newest_packages()[0]
>>> package.name
'yarg'
>>> package = yarg.latest_updated_packages()[0]
>>> package.name
'yarg'
```

methods.name: yarg.parse.Package.url
documentation:
This is only available for {meth}`yarg.latest_updated_packages`, for
{meth}`yarg.newest_packages` will return `None`

> ```pycon
> >>> package = yarg.latest_updated_packages()[0]
> >>> package.url
> 'http://pypi.python.org/pypi/yarg'
> ```

methods.name: yarg.parse.Package.date
documentation:
```pycon
>>> package = yarg.newest_packages()[0]
>>> package.date
datetime.datetime(2014, 8, 9, 8, 40, 20)
>>> package = yarg.latest_updated_packages()[0]
>>> package.date
datetime.datetime(2014, 8, 9, 8, 40, 20)
```

methods.name: yarg.parse.Package.description
documentation:
```pycon
>>> package = yarg.newest_packages()[0]
>>> package.description
'Some random summary stuff'
>>> package = yarg.latest_updated_packages()[0]
>>> package.description
'Some random summary stuff'
```

A PyPI package generated from the RSS feed information.

```
param pypi_dict

A dictionary retrieved from the PyPI server.
```

classes.name: yarg.release.Release

methods.name: yarg.release.Release.release_id
documentation:
```pycon
>>> package = yarg.get('yarg')
>>> v = "0.1.0"
>>> r = package.release(v)
>>> r[0].release_id
'0.1.0'
```

methods.name: yarg.release.Release.uploaded
documentation:
```pycon
>>> package = yarg.get('yarg')
>>> v = "0.1.0"
>>> r = package.release(v)
>>> r.uploaded
datetime.datime(2014, 8, 7, 21, 26, 19)
```


documentation:

```pycon
>>> r = package.release(v)
>>> r.has_sig

```

A release file from PyPI.

```
param release_id

A release id.

param pypi_dict

A dictionary of a release file.
```

functions.name: yarg.package.json2package
documentation:
Returns a {class}`yarg.release.Release` object from JSON content from the
PyPI server.

```
param json_content

JSON encoded content from the PyPI server.
```

```pycon
>>> package.downloads
Downloads(day=50100, week=367941, month=1601938)  # I wish
```

methods.name: yarg.package.Package.classifiers
documentation:
```pycon
>>> package = yarg.get('yarg')
>>> package.classifiers
['License :: OSI Approved :: MIT License',
 'Programming Language :: Python :: 2.7',
 'Programming Language :: Python :: 3.4']
```

methods.name: yarg.package.Package.python_versions
documentation:
Returns a list of Python version strings that
the package has listed in {attr}`yarg.Release.classifiers`.

> ```pycon
> >>> package = yarg.get('yarg')
> >>> package.python_versions
> ['2.6', '2.7', '3.3', '3.4']
> ```

methods.name: yarg.package.Package.python_implementations
documentation:
Returns a list of Python implementation strings that
the package has listed in {attr}`yarg.Release.classifiers`.

> ```pycon
> >>> package = yarg.get('yarg')
> >>> package.python_implementations
> ['CPython', 'PyPy']
> ```

methods.name: yarg.package.Package.latest_release_id
documentation:
```pycon
>>> package = yarg.get('yarg')
>>> package.latest_release_id
'0.1.0'
```

methods.name: yarg.package.Package.latest_release
documentation:
A list of {class}`yarg.release.Release` objects for each file in the
latest release.

> ```pycon
> >>> package = yarg.get('yarg')
> >>> package.latest_release
> [<Release 0.1.0>, <Release 0.1.0>]
> ```

methods.name: yarg.package.Package.has_wheel
documentation:
Returns `True` if one of the {class}`yarg.release.Release` objects
in the latest set of release files is `wheel` format. Returns
`False` if not.

> ```pycon
> >>> package = yarg.get('yarg')
> >>> package.has_wheel
> True
> ```

methods.name: yarg.package.Package.has_egg
documentation:
Returns `True` if one of the {class}`yarg.release.Release` objects
in the latest set of release files is `egg` format. Returns
`False` if not.

> ```pycon
> >>> package = yarg.get('yarg')
> >>> package.has_egg
> False
> ```

methods.name: yarg.package.Package.has_source
documentation:
Returns `True` if one of the {class}`yarg.release.Release` objects
in the latest set of release files is `source` format. Returns
`False` if not.

> ```pycon
> >>> package = yarg.get('yarg')
> >>> package.has_source
> True
> ```

methods.name: yarg.package.Package.release_ids
documentation:
```pycon
>>> package = yarg.get('yarg')
>>> package.release_ids
['0.0.1', '0.0.5', '0.1.0']
```

methods.name: yarg.package.Package.release
documentation:
A list of {class}`yarg.release.Release` objects for each file in a
release.

```
param release_id

A pypi release id.

>>> package = yarg.get('yarg')
>>> last_release = yarg.releases[-1]
>>> package.release(last_release)
[<Release 0.1.0>, <Release 0.1.0>]
```

A PyPI package.

```
param pypi_dict

A dictionary retrieved from the PyPI server.
```
